what: CRAN / RStudio Addin Engineering Specification — v0.1 to v1.0
title: FALSIFYR
text: Working package name: falsifyrCore action: attack(model, term = "treatment")One-line pitch: falsifyr takes fitted R model test result, automatically attacks statistical claim plausible alternative assumptions, tells smallest reasonable change make claim disappear.

title: FALSIFYR
text: statistical tools help confirm, diagnose, report, visualize model. falsifyr opposite. asks: take kill result? normal R workflow often ends something like: analyst sees: looks publishable, reportable, least exciting. real question merely whether p-value 0.05. real question : claim survive robust standard errors? survive bootstrap resampling? survive removing influential rows? survive small measurement error? survive reasonable missing-data alternatives? survive plausible covariate changes? survive placebo outcomes fake treatment labels? survive alternate train/test splits? smallest plausible perturbation makes die? falsifyr turns one command: Example output: feel less like linter like controlled demolition system statistical claims.
code: model <- lm(score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score, data = df) summary(model) treatment estimate = 0.42 p = 0.018 library(falsifyr)  fit <- lm(score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score, data = df)  attack(fit, term = "treatment") FALSIFYR CLAIM ATTACK REPORT  Claim:   treatment -> score  Original:   estimate = 0.42   p = 0.018  Survival:   38 / 100  (FRAGILE)  Smallest kill:   Remove 3 influential rows -> p = 0.091  Most lethal assumption:   Complete-case missingness.   Median imputation changes p = 0.018 -> p = 0.073.  Attack summary:   ✅ survives bootstrap resampling   ✅ survives HC3 robust standard errors   ⚠ dies when baseline_score is removed   ⚠ dies under 2.5% outcome noise   ❌ dies under median imputation   ❌ placebo outcome produces similarly strong effects in 13% of simulations  Verdict:   The claim exists only under a narrow analytical path.

title: FALSIFYR
text: falsifyr : Chaos Monkey statistical claims. software engineering, chaos testing deliberately breaks systems see whether resilient. falsifyr deliberately perturbs assumptions, rows, covariates, standard errors, missing-data rules, labels see whether R claim survives.

title: FALSIFYR
text: falsifyr : general model diagnostics package; multiverse analysis authoring framework; p-hacking detector; fraud detector; replacement subject-matter expertise; causal-inference identification checker; automatic referee declares results true false; package requires user hand-declare every robustness specification.

title: FALSIFYR
text: wedge “robustness checks.” already exists conceptually. wedge : Given fitted object term/claim, automatically search smallest plausible attack makes claim disappear. Existing tools usually ask analyst say: falsifyr says:
code: Here are the alternative specs I want to run. Give me the final model. I will attack it.

title: FALSIFYR
text: package must built adjacent ecosystem mind. becomes “robustness analysis package,” special enough. spec deliberately positions falsifyr around automated adversarial search claims, around user-authored multiverse analysis one narrow sensitivity method.

title: FALSIFYR
text: falsifyr place declare every possible analysis. tool looks result already believe asks, “weakest assumption holding ?”

title: FALSIFYR
text: MVP huge. MVP must one thing well demo lands 30 seconds: return: Original claim estimate, standard error, statistic, p-value, confidence interval. set attacks. ranked list attacks “kill distance.” smallest kill. survival score. concise console report. plot. optional RStudio Addin report. MVP produce dramatic “smallest kill” lm() glm(), nothing else matters.
code: fit <- lm(score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score, data = trial) attack(fit, term = "treatment")

text: “ran regression class/project/report. want know significant result fragile submit .” Workflow: Value: Learns assumptions matter. Gets intuitive robustness explanation. need deep sensitivity-analysis knowledge.
code: fit <- lm(outcome ~ treatment + x1 + x2, data = df) attack(fit, term = "treatment")

title: FALSIFYR
text: “need stress-test observational result sending PI.” Workflow: Value: Shows omitted/covariate fragility. Tests missingness robust SE choices. Finds influential hospitals/patients grouped data provided.
code: fit <- glm(readmission ~ intervention + age + sex + comorbidity,            data = hospital,            family = binomial())  attack(fit, term = "intervention", profile = "clinical")

title: FALSIFYR
text: “Someone sent model result. want accuse anything; want know make result fail.” Workflow: Value: Non-accusatory language. Evidence-linked claim fragility. Clear “survives/dies” table.
code: attack(fit, term = "exposure", intensity = "normal") report(result, "claim_attack.html")

title: FALSIFYR
text: “Show RStudio Addin turns boring regression dramatic claim survival report.” Workflow: Fit model RStudio. Highlight fit select environment. Addins → Attack Claim Choose term. Viewer opens survival report.

title: FALSIFYR
code: attack <- function(   model,   term = NULL,   data = NULL,   outcome = NULL,   profile = c("default", "clinical", "social_science", "prediction", "strict", "fast"),   attacks = NULL,   intensity = c("fast", "normal", "deep", "insane"),   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = c("p_over_alpha", "ci_crosses_zero", "sign_flip", "effect_below_threshold"),   effect_threshold = NULL,   seed = 1,   parallel = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: FALSIFYR
text: Return S3 object class: Structure:
code: class(result) # c("falsifyr_attack", "list") result <- list(   claim = list(     model_class = "lm",     formula = score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score,     outcome = "score",     term = "treatment",     estimate = 0.42,     std_error = 0.16,     statistic = 2.56,     p_value = 0.018,     conf_low = 0.10,     conf_high = 0.74,     alpha = 0.05,     kill_rule = "p_over_alpha"   ),   attacks = tibble::tibble(     attack_id,     attack_family,     attack_name,     status,     killed,     estimate,     p_value,     delta_p,     delta_estimate,     kill_distance,     severity,     explanation,     payload   ),   smallest_kill = list(...),   survival_score = 38,   verdict = "FRAGILE",   runtime = list(...),   warnings = list(...) )

title: FALSIFYR
text: Example:
code: print.falsifyr_attack <- function(x, ...) {   # Use cli for attractive console output. } ══ FALSIFYR ATTACK ═════════════════════════════════════  Claim   term: treatment   estimate: 0.42   p-value: 0.018  Verdict   FRAGILE  |  survival score: 38/100  Smallest kill   Row deletion attack:   remove 3 rows -> p = 0.091  Weakest assumptions   1. Missing-data rule   2. Dependence on baseline_score   3. Influential observations  Use plot(x) for survival map. Use report(x, "attack.html") for full report.

title: FALSIFYR
text: Plot ideas: survival bar; attack family heatmap; p-value distribution across attacks; “smallest kill” staircase; perturbation curve measurement error; covariate-dependency tornado plot; row-deletion fragility curve.
code: plot(result)

title: FALSIFYR
text: Report sections: Claim card. Survival score. Smallest kill. Attack leaderboard. Family--family details. Model/data limitations. Reproducibility appendix: package versions, seed, call, runtime, attack settings.
code: report(result, file = "falsifyr_report.html")

title: FALSIFYR
text: claim whole model. claim specific estimand/result extracted model test. Examples: coefficient treatment lm; odds ratio intervention glm(family = binomial); difference means t.test; hazard ratio term coxph; AUC RMSE difference predictive model later. Claim fields:
code: claim <- list(   estimand_type = "coefficient",   term = "treatment",   direction = "positive",   estimate = 0.42,   uncertainty = "std_error",   p_value = 0.018,   alpha = 0.05,   kill_rule = "p_over_alpha" )

title: FALSIFYR
text: attack controlled perturbation changes one assumption, data subset, estimator, SE method, validation choice. attack must : reproducible; labeled; explainable; bounded plausibility settings; able return comparable claim.

title: FALSIFYR
text: kill occurs perturbed claim fails chosen rule. Default kill rule: kill rules: confidence interval crosses zero; sign flips; effect size practical threshold; prediction metric falls threshold; clinical/practical conclusion changes.
code: p_value > alpha

title: FALSIFYR
text: smaller perturbation needed kill claim, fragile claim. Examples: row deletion kill distance = number/proportion rows removed; noise kill distance = noise SD relative outcome SD; missingness kill distance = imputation method severity; covariate kill distance = one-covariate removal/addition; placebo kill distance = frequency placebo claims strong original.

title: FALSIFYR
text: rough 0–100 score summarizing claim robustness. pretend universal scientific truth. communication device. Possible formula: Verdicts: Every report must say: survival score heuristic summary, formal probability claim true.
code: survival_score =   100   - weighted_penalty(smallest_kill)   - weighted_penalty(number_of_attack_families_that_kill)   - weighted_penalty(placebo_rate)   + bonus(consistency_across_robust_methods)

title: FALSIFYR
text: Purpose: determine whether claim depends fragile uncertainty estimator. Initial attacks lm/glm: Classical SE vs HC0/HC1/HC2/HC3 robust SE. Cluster-robust SE later, cluster variable supplied. Bootstrap CI/p-value approximation. Permutation test simple treatment/exposure claims. Example output: Implementation options: base stats; optional sandwich lmtest Suggests Imports; bootstrap can implemented internally. MVP: implement classical + bootstrap internally; optionally support sandwich installed.
code: HC3 robust SE:   p = 0.018 -> 0.061   killed: yes   explanation: The claim depends on classical homoskedastic SEs.

title: FALSIFYR
text: Purpose: find smallest set observations whose removal kills claim. Levels: Single-row deletion scan. Greedy k-row deletion. Influence-ranked deletion Cook’s distance / leverage / DFBETAs. Group deletion cluster/group variable supplied. MVP algorithm: Defaults: Output: Important safety wording: “rows influential” “rows removed.” “Smallest kill attack” “proof result invalid.”
code: 1. Fit original model. 2. Compute influence measures where possible. 3. Rank rows by absolute influence on target term. 4. Iteratively remove top k rows. 5. Refit model and extract claim after each k. 6. Stop when kill rule triggers or max deletion reached. max_remove_prop = 0.05 max_remove_n = min(20, floor(0.05 * n)) Smallest kill: remove 3 rows. Rows: 18, 44, 102. These rows have high leverage and all favor the claimed direction.

title: FALSIFYR
text: Purpose: identify whether claim exists narrow covariate set. Attacks: Drop-one-covariate analysis. Add-one-covariate analysis supplied candidate variables. Swap transformations: raw/log/standardized numeric covariates. Interaction toggle obvious treatment × subgroup interactions later. MVP: Output: Need careful interpretation. Covariate dependence inherently bad. many causal designs, adjustment correct. package say: attack tests dependence, correctness. claim can legitimately depend theoretically necessary covariate.
code: attack(fit, term = "treatment", covariates = "drop_one") Claim dies when baseline_score is removed:   p = 0.018 -> 0.142  Interpretation:   The result is highly dependent on adjustment for baseline_score.

title: FALSIFYR
text: Purpose: determine whether claim depends one missing-data handling choice. Attacks: Complete-case vs mean imputation. Complete-case vs median imputation. Missingness indicator predictors. Simple regression imputation later. Multiple imputation later, likely MVP due complexity. MVP constraints: numeric predictors mean/median impute; factors imputed mode explicit "(Missing)"; outcome missingness imputed default. Output:
code: Missing-data attack:   complete case p = 0.018   median imputation p = 0.073   killed: yes  Missingness imbalance:   controls removed: 18%   treated removed: 4%

title: FALSIFYR
text: Purpose: determine small measurement perturbations can kill claim. Attacks: Add Gaussian noise outcome. Add Gaussian noise key numeric covariates. Misclassification flip binary treatment/exposure. Label noise factor predictors later. Method: noise level, simulate B replicates. Refit model. Estimate kill probability noise level. Find smallest noise level kill probability exceeds threshold. Example: can dramatic intuitive.
code: Outcome noise attack:   Claim dies in 50% of simulations at noise SD = 0.13 * SD(outcome).

title: FALSIFYR
text: Purpose: detect whether similarly strong claims appear . Attacks: Permute treatment labels. Use placebo outcomes supplied user. Use future/past outcomes supplied. Random fake predictor distribution. Output: Important: placebos context-dependent. Default include label permutation statistically appropriate, placebo outcomes require user input.
code: Placebo attack:   13% of permuted treatment labels produced p-values as small as original.   interpretation: This weakens confidence in the claim.

title: FALSIFYR
text: Purpose: generate plausible model variations see often claim survives. family closest multiverse/specification-curve analysis, careful. falsifyr become specr. MVP: drop-one covariate; add candidate covariates; alternate transforms numeric variables; robust vs classical SE; huge combinatorial explosion default. Output: specr-style plot can optional, headline “survival” “smallest kill.”
code: Specification attack:   Claim survives 8 / 47 plausible specifications.   Median p-value across specifications: 0.064.

title: FALSIFYR
text: predictive models later. Attacks: random train/test split; stratified split; grouped split; temporal split; leakage suspicion performance collapses grouped/temporal split. family MVP unless supporting predictive/tidymodels workflows.

title: FALSIFYR
text: Support: lm glm gaussian/binomial t.test wilcox.test cor.test Minimum feature set: Extract claim. Refit model perturbations. Attack row deletion, robust/bootstrapped SE, covariate drop-one, missingness simple, noise, placebo permutation. Print plot.

code: extract_claim <- function(model, term = NULL, ...) {   UseMethod("extract_claim") }  refit_model <- function(model, data, formula = NULL, ...) {   UseMethod("refit_model") }  run_attack <- function(attack, claim, model, data, ...) {   UseMethod("run_attack") }

title: FALSIFYR
text: core challenge robustly refitting original model perturbing data. lm glm, capture: Refit strategy: Avoid dangerous eval hacks possible. Handle offsets/weights carefully.

title: FALSIFYR
code: extract_claim.lm <- function(model, term, alpha = 0.05, ...) {   coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients   if (is.null(term)) term <- choose_default_term(coefs)   row <- coefs[term, , drop = FALSE]   ci <- stats::confint(model, parm = term, level = 1 - alpha)   list(     term = term,     estimate = unname(row[1, "Estimate"]),     std_error = unname(row[1, "Std. Error"]),     statistic = unname(row[1, "t value"]),     p_value = unname(row[1, "Pr(>|t|)"]),     conf_low = unname(ci[1]),     conf_high = unname(ci[2])   ) }

title: FALSIFYR
text: Handle coefficient table column names: "z value" "Pr(>|z|)" common GLM. Use family(model)$family family(model)$link.

title: FALSIFYR
text: t.test, wilcox.test, cor.test, claim may named coefficient. Fields: Attacks htest can limited first.
code: list(   term = "test",   estimate = if present,   statistic = object$statistic,   p_value = object$p.value,   conf_low = object$conf.int[1],   conf_high = object$conf.int[2],   method = object$method )

title: FALSIFYR
text: Pseudo-code: Ranking options: DFBETAs target term available. Cook’s distance fallback. Absolute residual × leverage fallback.
code: attack_row_deletion <- function(model, claim, max_remove_n = 20, max_remove_prop = 0.05) {   data <- model.frame(model)   n <- nrow(data)   max_k <- min(max_remove_n, floor(max_remove_prop * n))    influence_rank <- rank_rows_by_target_influence(model, claim$term)    results <- vector("list", max_k)    for (k in seq_len(max_k)) {     remove_idx <- influence_rank[seq_len(k)]     data_k <- data[-remove_idx, , drop = FALSE]     fit_k <- refit_model(model, data_k)     claim_k <- extract_claim(fit_k, term = claim$term)     results[[k]] <- summarize_attack(...)     if (is_killed(claim_k)) break   }    bind_results(results) }

title: FALSIFYR
text: expensive cooler: “adversarial” simply removing top Cook’s distance rows. Default normal/deep mode: fast: influence-ranked deletion normal: greedy among top 20 rows deep: greedy among top 50 rows insane: beam search row subsets
code: At each step:   for each remaining row candidate among top M influence rows:     remove row temporarily     refit     measure movement toward kill   permanently remove row that most increases p-value / moves CI toward null Stop when killed.

title: FALSIFYR
text: Pseudo-code: Report: kill probability curve; noise level 50% kill rate; first level median p > alpha.
code: for (level in noise_grid) {   for (b in seq_len(B)) {     data_b <- data     data_b[[outcome]] <- data[[outcome]] + rnorm(n, 0, level * sd(data[[outcome]], na.rm = TRUE))     fit_b <- refit_model(model, data_b)     claim_b <- extract_claim(fit_b, term)     killed[b] <- is_killed(claim_b)   }   kill_rate[level] <- mean(killed) }

title: FALSIFYR
text: Pseudo-code: Need preserve row alignment carefully.
code: data_original <- recover_original_data(model) data_complete <- model.frame(model)  methods <- c("complete_case", "mean_impute", "median_impute", "mode_factor", "missing_indicator")  for method in methods:   data_m <- apply_missingness_strategy(data_original, method)   fit_m <- refit_model(model, data_m)   claim_m <- extract_claim(fit_m, term)

title: FALSIFYR
text: Pseudo-code: requires mapping term back variable, nontrivial factors/interactions. MVP can support simple non-factor numeric/binary terms first.
code: for (b in seq_len(B)) {   data_b <- data   data_b[[term_variable]] <- sample(data_b[[term_variable]])   fit_b <- refit_model(model, data_b)   claim_b <- extract_claim(fit_b, term)   placebo_stronger[b] <- claim_b$p_value <= original$p_value &&                          abs(claim_b$estimate) >= abs(original$estimate) } placebo_rate <- mean(placebo_stronger)

code: library(falsifyr)  fit <- lm(score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score, data = fragile_trial)  res <- attack(   fit,   term = "treatment",   intensity = "normal",   profile = "social_science" )  res plot(res) report(res, "attack.html") res <- attack(   fit,   term = "treatment",   attacks = c("row_deletion", "robust_se", "missingness", "noise", "placebo"),   kill_rule = "ci_crosses_zero",   parallel = TRUE,   seed = 42 ) res <- attack(   fit,   term = "intervention",   profile = "clinical",   kill_rule = "effect_below_threshold",   effect_threshold = log(1.10) )

title: FALSIFYR
text: Many fitted R model objects contain model frame used, necessarily full original data unused columns. Covariate-addition attacks may require data = supplied explicitly. Policy: attacks requiring extra data, ask data. silently use parent-frame evaluation unless safe. Report unavailable attacks clearly.

title: FALSIFYR
text: completed version lets author truthfully say: built CRAN-native RStudio package turns fitted model adversarial robustness test. merely diagnose model; searches smallest plausible perturbation changes conclusion, across influence, robust uncertainty, missing data, measurement error, placebo tests, specification choices. produces claim survival report makes statistical fragility visible ordinary R users. stronger identity : “built another R assistant”; “built linter”; “built dashboard”; “built generic sensitivity package.” memorable command : Everything else exists make command feel insane.
code: attack(model, term = "treatment")

title: FALSIFYR
text: Current adjacent tools inspect implementing: specr: CRAN page says provides utilities specification curve multiverse analyses, including setup, running, evaluation, plotting specifications. URL: https://cran.r-project.org/package=specr multiverse: CRAN page says implements multiverse-style analyses users declare alternative analysis branches R R Notebooks. URL: https://cran.r-project.org/package=multiverse sensemakr: CRAN page says implements sensitivity analysis tools extending omitted-variable-bias frameworks regression models. URL: https://cran.r-project.org/package=sensemakr tipr: CRAN page says conducts tipping-point analyses around unmeasured confounding result may tip insignificance. URL: https://cran.r-project.org/package=tipr influence.: CRAN page says detects influential cases generalized mixed-effects models includes procedure detect changing levels significance. URL: https://cran.r-project.org/package=influence.performance: CRAN page says provides model-quality utilities diagnostic checks large variety regression models. URL: https://cran.r-project.org/package=performance Codex keep researching space implementation update docs/differentiation.md whenever finds closer competitor.

title: Adversarial Robustness Attacks for Statistical Claims
text: 1.0 engine searches across: influence-ranked, greedy, beam, grouped row deletion; bootstrap uncertainty optional HC0-HC3 robust standard errors; drop-one covariate dependence bounded specification changes; mean, median, factor/mode, missingness-indicator alternatives; outcome/predictor noise binary label flips; label permutation, fake predictors, supplied placebo outcomes; random stratified split stability. intensity = "fast", "normal", "deep", "insane" controls search breadth. parallel = TRUE runs independent attack families two workers. stochastic attacks deterministic fixed seed. Grouped deletion enabled observations belong meaningful units:
code: attack(   fit,   term = "treatment",   data = trial_data,   cluster = "site",   attacks = "row_deletion" )

Read every verdict together attacks run report’s limitations.

title: Adversarial Robustness Attacks for Statistical Claims
text: Existing tools help define, diagnose, run robustness analyses. falsifyr automatically attacks fitted claim searches smallest plausible perturbation changes conclusion.

title: Attack a statistical claim — attack
text: Runs collection adversarial robustness checks fitted model claim. returned object summarizes whether claim survives attack, smallest perturbation kills , overall survival score.

title: Attack a statistical claim — attack
code: attack(   model,   term = NULL,   data = NULL,   outcome = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   profile = c("default", "clinical", "social_science", "prediction", "strict", "fast"),   attacks = NULL,   intensity = c("normal", "fast", "deep", "insane"),   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = c("p_over_alpha", "ci_crosses_zero", "sign_flip", "effect_below_threshold"),   effect_threshold = NULL,   seed = 1,   parallel = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Attack a statistical claim — attack
text: model fitted lm, glm, aov, lme4::lmer, lme4::glmer, survival::coxph model. htest objects return explicit limited-support result. term Character scalar naming coefficient test term attack. NULL, falsifyr attacks first non-intercept coefficient. data Optional data frame used refit model. omitted, falsifyr attempts recover model data. outcome Optional character vector user-supplied placebo outcome names placebo attack family. cluster Optional character scalar naming grouping variable grouped row-deletion attack. Supply data grouping variable part fitted formula. profile Character scalar choosing attack profile. Profiles tune default attack-family emphasis attacks = NULL; profile = "fast" also defaults intensity = "fast" intensity supplied. attacks Character vector attack families. NULL runs default families. intensity Character scalar controlling attack breadth: "fast", "normal", "deep", "insane". alpha Significance level used kill rules. alternative Character scalar defining claim direction coefficient tests: "two.sided", "less", "greater". kill_rule Character scalar defining kills claim. Supported rules "p_over_alpha", "ci_crosses_zero", "sign_flip", "effect_below_threshold". effect_threshold Numeric threshold used "effect_below_threshold". seed Integer seed deterministic attack runs. parallel Logical; TRUE, independent attack families run two local workers. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages expensive "insane" attack runs.

title: Attack a statistical claim — attack
text: falsifyr_attack object extracted claim, attack leaderboard, smallest kill, survival score, verdict, runtime metadata, warnings.

title: Attack a statistical claim — attack
code: fit <- lm(score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score, data = fragile_trial) result <- attack(fit, term = "treatment", attacks = "row_deletion", intensity = "fast") result #>  #> -- FALSIFYR ATTACK ------------------------------------------------------------- #> Claim #> treatment -> score #> formula: score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score #> estimate: 0.443 #> p-value: 0.041 #> confidence interval: [0.0251, 0.861] #>  #> Verdict #> MIXED | survival score: 47/100 #>  #> Smallest kill #> Influential row deletion: remove 1 row -> p = 0.069 #> Rows: 43 #> Method: ranked #>  #> Weakest assumptions #> 1. Row deletion / influence: remove 1 row -> p = 0.069 #>  #> Died under #> Influential row deletion #>  #> Survived #> none #>  #> A killed claim is fragile under an attack; that does not prove it is false. #> Use plot(x) for survival map. #> Use report(x, "attack.html") for full report.

title: Extract the attack leaderboard — attack_leaderboard

title: Extract the attack leaderboard — attack_leaderboard
code: attack_leaderboard(result)

title: Extract the attack leaderboard — attack_leaderboard
text: result falsifyr_attack object returned attack().

title: Extract the attack leaderboard — attack_leaderboard
text: tibble ranked attack results.

title: Extract the attack leaderboard — attack_leaderboard
code: fit <- lm(score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score, data = fragile_trial) result <- attack(fit, term = "treatment", attacks = "row_deletion", intensity = "fast") attack_leaderboard(result) #> # A tibble: 1 x 14 #>   attack_id     attack_family attack_name status killed estimate p_value delta_p #>   <chr>         <chr>         <chr>       <chr>  <lgl>     <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 row_deletion~ row_deletion  Influentia~ killed TRUE      0.355  0.0694  0.0280 #> # i 6 more variables: delta_estimate <dbl>, kill_distance <dbl>, #> #   severity <dbl>, explanation <chr>, payload <list>, leaderboard_rank <int>

what: Extract the statistical claim from a model — extract_claim
title: Extract the statistical claim from a model — extract_claim
text: Builds claim card falsifyr attacks: term, estimate, uncertainty, p-value, confidence interval, kill-rule metadata.

title: Extract the statistical claim from a model — extract_claim
code: extract_claim(model, term = NULL, ...)  # Default S3 method extract_claim(model, term = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'lm' extract_claim(   model,   term = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = "p_over_alpha",   effect_threshold = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'glm' extract_claim(   model,   term = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = "p_over_alpha",   effect_threshold = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'htest' extract_claim(   model,   term = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = "p_over_alpha",   effect_threshold = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'anova' extract_claim(   model,   term = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = "p_over_alpha",   effect_threshold = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'aov' extract_claim(   model,   term = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = "p_over_alpha",   effect_threshold = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'merMod' extract_claim(   model,   term = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = "p_over_alpha",   effect_threshold = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'coxph' extract_claim(   model,   term = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   kill_rule = "p_over_alpha",   effect_threshold = NULL,   ... )

title: Extract the statistical claim from a model — extract_claim

Additional arguments passed methods. alpha Significance level stored extracted claim. alternative Character scalar defining claim direction coefficient tests: "two.sided", "less", "greater". kill_rule Character scalar naming kill rule store extracted claim. effect_threshold Numeric threshold stored claim "effect_below_threshold".

code: fit <- lm(score ~ treatment + age + baseline_score, data = fragile_trial) result <- attack(fit, term = "treatment", attacks = "row_deletion", intensity = "fast") smallest_kill(result) #> $attack_id #> [1] "row_deletion_influential_row_deletion" #>  #> $attack_family #> [1] "row_deletion" #>  #> $attack_name #> [1] "Influential row deletion" #>  #> $status #> [1] "killed" #>  #> $killed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $estimate #> [1] 0.3553001 #>  #> $p_value #> [1] 0.06939223 #>  #> $delta_p #> [1] 0.02798691 #>  #> $delta_estimate #> [1] -0.08749806 #>  #> $kill_distance #> [1] 0.01351351 #>  #> $severity #> [1] 35 #>  #> $explanation #> [1] "remove 1 row -> p = 0.069" #>  #> $payload #> $payload[[1]] #> $payload[[1]]$rows #> [1] 43 #>  #> $payload[[1]]$rows_removed #> [1] 1 #>  #> $payload[[1]]$method #> [1] "ranked" #>  #> $payload[[1]]$curve #>   k rows_removed rows  estimate    p_value killed   progress #> 1 1            1   43 0.3553001 0.06939223   TRUE 0.06939223 #>  #>  #>  #> $leaderboard_rank #> [1] 1 #>

title: Releasing falsifyr
text: Run devtools::document() devtools::test() current R. Run GitHub-corpus source("validation/run-validation.R"). Run lintr::lint_package() build pkgdown site. Build source tarball R CMD build .. Run R CMD check ---cran tarball working LaTeX toolchain. Check tarball R-devel major CRAN platforms. Update cran-comments.md, submit source tarball CRAN form, confirm maintainer email. submit another build CRAN submission pending.

title: falsifyr 1.0.0
text: Added claim-first attack(model, term) engine ranked smallest-kill output. Added row deletion (ranked, greedy, beam, grouped), uncertainty, covariate, missingness, measurement-error, placebo, specification, split attack families. Added support lm, glm, aov, lmerMod, glmerMod, coxph perturbation workflows, plus explicit limited support htest anova objects retain refittable source data. Added deterministic fast/normal/deep/insane intensity profiles, claim attack registries, two-worker parallel family execution. Added survival scoring, verdicts, smallest-kill leaderboard accessors, console/plot output, standalone HTML reports, RStudio addin. Added synthetic fragile/resilient data, vignettes, GitHub-validation corpus, cross-platform R CMD check automation.
